sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the blue blob didn't seem to change much - the board changed but the nodes look essentially the same. The only difference I see is node 7 (black background) now has 3317 pixels instead of 3315, and node 17 (white border) has 62 instead of 64. This is likely just the HUD/timer changing.
Let me reconsider. The sky blue dots are scattered around. Maybe I need to click on them in some order. Or maybe I need to click on the colored squares at the top to select a color, then click on something else.
Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking the blue blob didn't change gameplay meaningfully
# Let me try clicking on a sky blue dot instead
# Sky blue dots at: (37,18), (37,41), (40,37), (41,16), (54,49), (55,14), (56,47), (57,16)

# Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 18}])
print(f"Result: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\nSky blue nodes after click:")
for i, n in enumerate(nodes):
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"  {i}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("\nAll nodes:")
for i, n in enumerate(nodes):
    print(f"  {i}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
